name: ai-tool-system-prompts
why_selected: Most comprehensive AI tool prompt collection. Daily updates. Full competitor internals from Augment Code, Claude Code, Cursor, Devin, Kiro, Lovable, Manus, Perplexity, Replit, Windsurf, v0, Xcode and 16+ more.
why_not_alternative: Only covers 4 models (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok). No tool-level prompts. Infrequent updates. Missing coding tool coverage.

name: general-prompt-library
why_selected: World's largest open-source prompt library (143K stars). Academic reference (Harvard, Columbia). Multi-platform (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral).
why_not_alternative: Closed platform. No git integration. Cannot be submoduled. Content quality unverified.

name: coding-tool-prompts
content_type: AI coding tool system prompts + definitions
why_selected: Date-stamped system prompt archive. Per-tool definitions for Augment, Claude Code, Cursor, Devin, Kiro, Gemini, Codex, Aider, Lovable, Qoder. Version tracking.
why_not_alternative: Scattered across vendor sites. No unified archive. No version comparison possible.

name: claude-code-internals
content_type: Claude Code system prompt parts + 24 tool defs
why_selected: Complete Claude Code system prompt breakdown. 24 built-in tool definitions. Sub-agent prompts. Version-tracked per Claude Code release.
why_not_alternative: Fragile approach. Breaks on updates. This repo is maintained and version-tracked.

name: prompt-engineering-guide
why_selected: Comprehensive prompt engineering guide (73K stars). Website + Jupyter notebooks. Covers all major techniques including chain-of-thought, few-shot, zero-shot, RAG, context engineering.
why_not_alternative: Less code-focused. No git repo to submodule. Website-only content.

name: bulk-prompt-collection
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - quantity over quality. Useful only for data mining.
why_not_alternative: Low-medium quality. No curation. Would add noise without filtering. Not worth the storage cost.

name: gpt-store-prompts
content_type: GPT Store custom GPT system prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - GPT-specific. Valuable for pattern analysis but not directly usable in Claude.
why_not_alternative: Platform-specific to ChatGPT/GPT Store. Prompt patterns are already covered by S07 and S11.

name: chatgpt-system-prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - primarily security/injection focused. Value is defensive awareness only.
why_not_alternative: Narrow focus. Security patterns already covered by S07. Not a primary component source.

name: top-gpt-store
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - academic/research angle. Multi-language but GPT-specific.
why_not_alternative: Same GPT-specific limitation. Multi-language adds complexity without Claude benefit.

name: anthropic-prompt-tutorial
why_selected: NOT INCLUDED as submodule - accessible via Anthropic docs website. Referenced in education materials.
why_not_alternative: Already available online. Not a component source. Used as reference material only.

name: templated-prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - experimental and template-based. Supports multiple providers but low adoption.
why_not_alternative: Low community adoption (1.6K stars). Experimental status. Template format adds complexity.

name: ai-coding-prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - framework-specific coding prompts. Covered better by S03 skills.
why_not_alternative: Skills from S03 (1370+) already cover all these frameworks with more depth and structure.

name: prompt-master-skill
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - single skill. Valuable but S01 already includes prompt-related skills.
why_not_alternative: Single skill doesn't justify a submodule. Equivalent functionality in S01 skills.

name: gpt-super-prompting
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - security research. Useful for defensive hardening but not a component source.
why_not_alternative: Primarily offensive security content. Defensive value covered by S07 prompt analysis.

name: big-prompt-library
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - comprehensive but mixed quality. Covers ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, Gemini, Cohere.
why_not_alternative: Mixed quality. Security perspective already covered by included sources. Storage cost not justified.

name: awesome-llm-prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - meta-index only. Used during research phase for discovery. No installable components.
why_not_alternative: Discovery tool, not a component source. Already used during initial repo scanning phase.

name: awesome-gpt-prompt-eng
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - resource list only. Same discovery role as PL-16.
why_not_alternative: Meta-index. No components. Already used during research. S13 covers education better.

name: curated-ai-system-prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - smaller subset of what S07 already covers. Agent builder focused.
why_not_alternative: S07 covers 28+ tools vs 11. Curated selection doesn't add enough unique value.

name: leaked-model-prompts
content_type: GPT-5.4, Claude Opus, Gemini 3.1, Grok system prompts
why_selected: NOT SELECTED - covers latest model prompts but narrow scope. S07 already tracks these.
why_not_alternative: Only 4 models. S07 tracks these plus 24+ more tools. Ethically questionable sourcing.
